title: AegisConfig
description:
Top-level Aegis runtime configuration.

Loaded in order: built-in defaults → `~/.config/aegis/config.toml` (user-global)
→ `.aegis.toml` (project). Later layers override ordinary scalar fields.
Project-local security-critical fields are ratcheted so they can only tighten,
never loosen: `mode`, `allowlist_override_level`, `ci_policy`,
`snapshot_policy`, `sandbox.enabled`/`required`/`allow_network`/`allow_write`,
and the `auto_snapshot_*` flags. `allow`/`block` rules are concatenated. See
ADR-013 for the full ordering.

allowlist_override_level.description: Maximum risk level the allow-list may auto-approve in Protect/Strict mode.

audit.description: Audit log rotation and integrity settings.

auto_snapshot_docker.description: Enable Docker when a command's Snapshot plan requests it.

auto_snapshot_git.description: Enable Git when a command's Snapshot plan requests it.

auto_snapshot_mysql.description: Enable MySQL when a command's Snapshot plan requests it.

auto_snapshot_postgres.description: Enable PostgreSQL when a command's Snapshot plan requests it.

auto_snapshot_sqlite.description: Enable SQLite when a command's Snapshot plan requests it.

auto_snapshot_supabase.description: Enable Supabase when a command's Snapshot plan requests it.

ci_policy.description: Behaviour when a CI environment is detected.

custom_patterns.description: Extra user-defined patterns merged with built-in patterns at runtime.

docker_scope.description: Which Docker containers to include in snapshots.

language_analysis.description: Language-aware analysis script-file and trusted-alias budgets.

mysql_snapshot.description: MySQL connection details used when snapshotting is enabled.

postgres_snapshot.description: PostgreSQL connection details used when snapshotting is enabled.

snapshot_policy.description: Controls which Snapshot plugins run for planned recovery.

sqlite_snapshot_path.description:
Path to a SQLite database file, relative to the current working
directory or absolute.

supabase_snapshot.description:
Supabase snapshot provider settings used when snapshotting is enabled.
Layered config replaces this provider config as a whole.

AllowlistOverrideLevel.description:
Maximum override level that structured allowlist rules may grant for
non-safe commands in Protect/Strict mode.

AllowlistRule.description: A structured allowlist rule with optional scope, expiry, and rationale.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for allowing this pattern.

AuditConfig.description: Audit log rotation and integrity configuration.

compress_rotated.description: Compress rotated audit files with gzip.

integrity_mode.description: Integrity chaining mode for corruption and inconsistent-edit checks.

max_file_size_bytes.description: Max audit file size in bytes before rotation.

retention_files.description: Number of rotated audit files to retain.

BlockRule.description: A structured block rule with optional scope, expiry, and rationale.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for blocking this pattern.

Category.description: Which class of operation the pattern guards against.

oneOf.description: File system operations (delete, overwrite, chmod, etc.).

CiPolicy.description:
What aegis does when it detects a CI environment.

`Block` is the safe default: no interactive TTY is available in CI, so
prompting would hang the pipeline.  Instead, non-safe commands are
hard-blocked and the pipeline fails fast with a clear error message.

`Allow` is an explicit opt-in override for cases where a project has
audited its CI pipeline and is confident that destructive commands are
intentional (e.g., a release script that runs `terraform destroy` in a
tear-down job).  Because a project-local `.aegis.toml` is untrusted, `Allow`
is only honored from the trusted global config
(`~/.config/aegis/config.toml`); a project cannot weaken an inherited
`Block` to `Allow` (see ADR-013).

oneOf.description: Hard-block all non-safe commands. No dialog. Pipeline fails fast.

Pass-through: commands run without prompting. Use only when you have
deliberately reviewed the CI pipeline for destructive commands.

DockerScope.description:
Scoping rules that decide *which* Docker containers are eligible for snapshot.

Stored under `[docker_scope]` in `aegis.toml`.

label.description:
Label selector for `Labeled` mode.  The container must carry this label
with value `"true"` to be eligible (e.g. `aegis.snapshot=true`).

name_patterns.description:
Name patterns for `Names` mode.  Each pattern is passed as a separate
`--filter name=<pat>` argument to `docker ps` (Docker ORs them).

DockerScopeMode.description:
Which Docker containers to include in snapshots.

- `Labeled` (default) — only containers with a specific label.
- `All`               — every running container (legacy blanket behaviour).
- `Names`             — containers whose name matches one of the given patterns.

oneOf.description: Only snapshot containers carrying the opt-in label (default).
Snapshot every running container — use with care on busy hosts.
Snapshot containers whose name matches at least one pattern.

LanguageAnalysisConfig.description:
Language-aware analysis script-file and trusted-alias budgets (ADR-022 §6).

`script_file_limit_bytes` is bounded by
[`LANGUAGE_ANALYSIS_SCRIPT_FILE_HARD_CEILING_BYTES`] at every layer; project
config may additionally only lower it, never raise it. `trusted_aliases` is
a Global-layer-only concept ("trusted global aliases only", ADR-022 §6) —
project-layer entries are dropped entirely rather than merged, since a
project must never be able to introduce a new trusted interpreter alias.

inline_source_limit_bytes.description: Maximum bytes accepted from one inline source body.

max_aggregate_bytes.description: Maximum aggregate source bytes across all accepted targets.

max_script_files.description: Maximum top-level script files inspected for one command.

max_targets.description: Maximum distinct top-level and recursive analysis targets.

script_file_limit_bytes.description: Maximum bytes read from a routed script file.

timeout_ms.description: Total wall-clock budget for language analysis of one command.

trusted_aliases.description:
Trusted global aliases mapping a wrapper program name to the canonical
registry interpreter it stands in for.

oneOf.description: Block non-safe and indirect execution by default.

MysqlSnapshotConfig.description:
Connection settings for a MySQL snapshot plugin.

Credentials are provided externally via `MYSQL_PWD` or `~/.my.cnf`.
The plugin is a no-op when [`database`](Self::database) is empty.

PolicyPatternToken.description:
A single token in a typed policy rule pattern.

- `Single(s)` matches exactly the literal string `s`.
- `Alts(v)` matches any one of the strings in `v`.

anyOf.description: A set of alternative tokens (any one must match).

PolicyRule.description:
A typed `[[rules]]` entry in `aegis.toml`.

Each rule defines a token-sequence pattern and the decision to apply when a
command matches.  Optional `match_examples` / `not_match_examples` let you
embed self-documenting test cases that are verified at config-load time.

decision.description: Decision when this rule fires and no `when` clause overrides it.

justification.description: Human-readable rationale stored in audit logs and shown in the UI.

match_examples.description: Example commands that MUST match this rule (validated at load time).

not_match_examples.description: Example commands that must NOT match this rule (validated at load time).

pattern.description: Ordered list of tokens the command must start with.

PolicyRuleDecision.description:
Decision produced by a typed `[[rules]]` policy entry in `aegis.toml`.

Serializes and deserializes as a snake_case string (`"allow"`, `"prompt"`,
`"block"`), but also accepts an internally-tagged map `{ decision = "allow" }`
when deserialized from a bare TOML document (used in unit tests).

PostgresSnapshotConfig.description:
Connection settings for a PostgreSQL snapshot plugin.

Credentials are provided externally via `PGPASSWORD` or `~/.pgpass`.
The plugin is a no-op when [`database`](Self::database) is empty.

enabled.description: Whether prune is enabled. Currently advisory; manual `--yes` is still required.

max_age_days.description: Keep snapshots newer than this many days. `None` disables the age rule.

max_count_per_provider.description: Keep the newest N snapshots per provider. `None` disables the count rule.

RiskLevel.description:
Classifies the risk level of a shell command.

Ordered by severity: `Safe < Warn < Danger < Block`.

`#[non_exhaustive]` ensures match arms in external crates require a wildcard,
preserving forward-compatibility if new levels are added in v2.

oneOf.description: No risk detected; command is safe to execute.
Never allow this command to run.

SandboxSettings.description: Sandbox configuration — controls whether commands run inside a bwrap sandbox.

allow_network.description: Whether the sandboxed process may access the network.

allow_write.description: Paths the sandboxed process is allowed to write to.

required.description: Fail hard if the sandbox cannot be set up (instead of falling back to unsandboxed exec).

SnapshotPolicy.description:
Controls when and how Snapshot plugins run for planned recovery.

- `None`      — never snapshot.
- `Selective` — only plugins enabled by `auto_snapshot_git` /
  `auto_snapshot_docker` / `auto_snapshot_postgres` /
  `auto_snapshot_mysql` / `auto_snapshot_supabase` /
  `auto_snapshot_sqlite`.
- `Full`      — run every registered plugin regardless of per-plugin flags.

SupabaseSnapshotConfig.description:
Configuration settings for the Supabase snapshot provider.

`project_ref` is advisory-only for audit/UI and future phases. The
rollback target-match setting is part of the bundle definition for later
rollback flows that compare the active config target with the manifest
target. Phase 1 uses the direct PostgreSQL transport in [`db`](Self::db).

db.description: Phase 1 direct PostgreSQL transport for Supabase snapshots.

project_ref.description: Advisory-only Supabase project reference for audit/UI and future phases.

require_config_target_match_on_rollback.description: Target-match preference for rollback flows that compare config targets.

TrustedAlias.description:
A trusted global alias for the Language-aware analysis interpreter
registry (ADR-022 §6) — e.g. a wrapper script name that should be treated
as a stand-in for a canonical registry interpreter.

alias.description: The alias program name as it appears in a command (e.g. `"py"`).

canonical.description: The canonical registry program name it stands in for (e.g. `"python3"`).

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this pattern detects.

justification.description: Optional rationale for adding this pattern.

risk.description: Risk level assigned when this pattern matches.

safe_alt.description: Safer alternative command to suggest, if any.

WhenClause.description:
Conditional override: when environment variable `env` equals `value`, use
`then` as the decision instead of the rule's default `decision`.

then.description: Decision to use when the condition is met.

value.description: Expected value of the environment variable.
